beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, light breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Ellie starts her Monday with full energy; a quick morning routine with espresso fuels her day. 35-minute beat is typical for her weekday wake-up.

beats.activity: Cycle from Roosevelt to Georgetown for work.
beats.reasoning: Ellie is a cyclist who values the weather; sunny Monday morning is perfect for a bike commute. 30 minutes is reasonable for Roosevelt to Georgetown on bike.

beats.activity: Morning site meetings and project planning at the construction office.
beats.reasoning: Ellie starts her workday early; she's a project manager who handles site coordination. She avoids the FIFA match (not her interest) and works through the morning.

beats.activity: Quick lunch grab (sandwich or salad) at a nearby deli.
beats.reasoning: Ellie grabs a fast lunch rather than sitting down; the FIFA match at 11am doesn't appeal to her competitive but conflict-averse nature—she'd rather stay focused on work.

beats.activity: Afternoon site walk-through, material inspections, and contractor check-ins.
beats.reasoning: Ellie continues her project management duties; afternoon is typically when site issues surface and need coordination. Light Rail closure doesn't affect her Georgetown location.

beats.activity: Cycle from Georgetown back toward home, stopping at a local market.
beats.reasoning: Ellie uses the beautiful afternoon to bike home via Wallingford; she stops to pick up groceries for the evening. 30 minutes accounts for the bike ride and quick shop.
Short final leg back to Roosevelt with her groceries. 30 minutes accounts for the ride and settling in.

beats.activity: Paint in home studio—working on a landscape piece.
beats.reasoning: Ellie values her painting hobby; sunny afternoon is prime time to work on her art. She's energized from a good day and uses this time to decompress creatively.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Ellie cooks a simple dinner with her groceries; she prefers intimate time with close friends or solo relaxation over big social events.

beats.activity: Video call with a close friend, then light reading on the couch.
beats.reasoning: Ellie values quality time with close friends; a video call satisfies her social needs without the crowd energy. She winds down with reading afterward.

beats.activity: Evening wind-down: tea, skincare routine, journaling, and preparing for bed.
beats.reasoning: Ellie establishes a calm evening routine; she's already home and ready for sleep. This beat ensures she's settled and relaxed before the final sleep beat.
Ellie gets 7.5 hours of solid sleep after a productive, fulfilling day. The sleep beat crosses midnight and ends before 06:30 as required.

diary: Woke up early with the sun streaming through the bedroom window—one of those perfect June mornings that makes you remember why Seattle's worth the rain. Cycled to Georgetown with my coffee still warm in my thermos, passing the bars already setting up for the World Cup match. Didn't even think about it, honestly. Work was the usual: site meetings, material checks, contractor calls. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I bike, but I heard people grumbling about it at the deli when I grabbed lunch. Stopped by QFC on the way home and picked up supplies for dinner. Spent the afternoon painting—something about the light hitting my studio made the landscape piece finally come together. Video called Maya around three, and we laughed about something stupid for twenty minutes. Felt good to just exist quietly after that, tea steaming beside me while I wrote in my journal. The day had a rhythm to it.
thought_process: I noticed the World Cup buzz downtown but it didn't pull at me—I'm not a big soccer person, and Monday mornings are sacred work time for me. The Light Rail closure was background noise; my bike route insulated me from the chaos. I think that's why today felt so grounded. Work was steady, nothing stressful, and I moved through it methodically. By afternoon, I was ready to shift into something creative. Painting has been my anchor lately, and I made real progress on that landscape. The video call with Maya reminded me I've been a bit isolated lately, so I welcomed that connection. Everything felt balanced—productive morning, nourishing afternoon, quiet evening. No big swings in mood, just contentment settling in as the day wound down.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
